channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Aston close: multiple road and pavement defects, 6th June
item.description:
Broken and uneven paving slabs, several areas of tarmac missing from the road, especially around drains and some previous repairs that are already crumbling.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aston Close, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking Access for Wheelchairs!, 6th June
item.description:
At the bottom end of the priory park where the so called new warehouse is underway they have fenced off the whole section unnecessarily so! I am currently in a wheelchair due to having an op and well I cannot get through that way which was the safest way for me to get into the main highstreet. People like myself with wheelchairs and others which pushchairs etc are having to go round the other way through the little gates by the old library which is incredibly dangerous due to the uneven ground! The park should be kept accessible for people at all times please! Also the grass is getting destroyed in the process too. Such ashame to ruin the park.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree pits, 6th June
item.description:
See 9570281 - 2 additional locations

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lost gravel from tree pits in Sidcup High street, 6th June
item.description:
Most of the original gravel has been lost from 4 tree pits. 2 photographs here to fix locations. 2 more in next report.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Smashed glass. Needs clearing, 5th June
item.description:
Glass has been smashed. Area made safe but glass needs clearing and panel replaced.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gravel Hill, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter

item.title: Bollard off its foundation, 5th June
item.description:
If remaining the bollards are enough to prevent vehicular access, it may suffice to remove this defective bollard rather than re-install it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Jenner Close, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Scrap timber in Knoll Road, 5th June
item.description:
Possible hazard to pedestrians and vehicles.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Knoll Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On newly developed verges pavements, 5th June
item.description:
Cars parked blocking entire footpath/ pavement area on the entrance to estate. As well as on verge on new development. Which was supposedly meant to incorporate more greenland than the open fields it originally was.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shoot Up Hill, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Defective street light outside 21 Elm Road, 5th June
item.description:
Street light not working on lamp post outside 21 Elm Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elm Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 5 Street Lamps dont work, 5th June
item.description:
This faulty street lamps all never work and the area it’s meant to light up is covered by greets and mini forest and a small hill. But it doesn’t light up making the area risk for crimes and safety issues. At night I normally hear arguements Dogs barking and people driving motorcycles fast and just fighting and the lights would help bring safety and security to that area. It has been like this for over months!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pickering Gardens, Friern Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead cat in road, 5th June
item.description:
Dead black cat on the A2 just after slip road from falconwood and just before stairs down onto Wincrofts drive
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Rochester Way, Avery Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Dead animal in the carriageway or footway

item.title: Graffiti on comms box on The Avenue, near Apex Close, 5th June
item.description:
The comms box between 44 The Avenue and Apex Close on the south side of The Avenue has some graffiti on it. Please can it be removed - thanks!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bike dumped, 5th June
item.description:
Dumped Forest bike on the footway. I do not believe that Forest bikes operate in the Greenwich borough

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Sherard Road, Well Hall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian footpaths/ pavements, 5th June
item.description:
Cars constantly parked on pedestrian footpaths, pavements blocking access for wheelchair users, those with pushchairs or those carrying loads.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Watling Gardens, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In the very middle of Belsize Terrace, centre of the road outside Aperivino bar or Mimi restaurant, 5th June
item.description:
There is water seeping through cracks in the tarmac, likely from a leaking pipe underneath. The tarmac is always wet, even in the dry weather.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10A, Belsize Terrace, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous stump, 5th June
item.description:
I am assuming this is going to be removed soon? Blocking the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shoreham Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Lime Bikes, 5th June
item.description:
These have fallen over in the wind A resident has already complained on 3rd June They are on footpath Point Hill/Maidenstone Hill but as of today they’ve been there a week! One bike plate is PPJEBS. This should help identify location. The resident was assured you would dispatch someone to collect? Unacceptable this has not happened

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maidenstone Hill, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed and rubbish, 5th June
item.description:
Bed and rubbish dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Windus Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 Canisters found in the parking area, 5th June
item.description:
2 canisters were found left on the ground in this parking area. We have moved them to the trash area of the apartments so they are not in the way of the cars, as this is seriously dangerous. We understand that this is not a way to dispose of it, but we don't know where these have come from so we don't feel comfortable taking it to the waste disposal area ourselves hence we want to let you know

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenhaven Drive, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shelf cupboard and supermarket trolley, 5th June
item.description:
Shelf cupboard and supermarket trolley were dumped here a few weeks ago.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bewick Mews, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
